summary: Comprehensive testing of UI/UX Cleanup & Financial Center Overhaul. All 22 backend tests passed. All frontend features verified via Playwright. Fixed a bug in ledger search where driver_name could be None causing 500 error.
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed: Ledger search API had a bug where driver_name could be None, causing AttributeError. Added null check before calling .lower() on driver_name.
seed_data_creation: Created test orders and payments during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: All UI/UX cleanup features verified working: 1) Boss Dashboard simplified - only stats cards + clickable Driver Holdings + Pending Deposits banner (no tabs). 2) Finance Center at /boss/ledger has two tabs: Pending Deposits (with approve/reject cards) and Transaction History (with search, type filter, driver filter, date range). 3) Pending Deposits banner navigates to /boss/ledger?tab=deposits. 4) Driver Holdings click navigates to /boss/ledger?driver_id=X&tab=history. 5) Driver Orders page has 'Mark as Done' button for pending orders (NOT photo upload). 6) Driver can mark their own orders as done via PUT /api/orders/{id}/complete. 7) Driver CANNOT mark other driver's orders as done (403). 8) Photo upload endpoints are REMOVED (POST /api/orders/{id}/photo returns 404). 9) Boss Orders and Service Orders pages do NOT have View Delivery Proof button. 10) Bottom nav shows 'Finance' (not 'Ledger') for boss role.

boss_dashboard_no_tabs.verification: Tabs element not found on dashboard page

finance_center_pending_deposits.verification: Deposit cards with Approve and Reject buttons visible

driver_holdings_navigation.verification: URL contains driver_id and tab=history, driver filter pre-selected

driver_orders_mark_as_done.verification: Mark as Done button visible and functional

bug_fixed.issue: Ledger search API returned 500 error when driver_name was None
bug_fixed.root_cause: The search filter code called .lower() on driver_name without checking for None
bug_fixed.fix: Added null check: driver_match = entry['driver_name'] and search_lower in entry['driver_name'].lower()
